document.name: browser-recording-test-showcase
document.title: Browser Recording Test (Playwright-MCP .webm)
document.summary: Minimal single-node test of the R1 persisted browser recording: a Playwright-MCP CLI critic agent launches a real headless Chromium, navigates to a URL, snapshots + screenshots it, then ends. The in-pod @playwright/mcp (--save-video) writes a .webm to /sandbox/work; cli-agent-py pushes it to the BFF browser-artifacts ingest at session finalize, where it renders inline on the run's Browser tab. Isolates browser/video capture from the heavier coding-redesign flow.

criticAgent.title: Playwright critic agent slug
criticAgent.description: cli-playwright-critic-agent (claude) | codex-playwright-critic-agent (codex). Must have the Playwright MCP server + --save-video.

agentConfig.name: Browser Recording Test
agentConfig.instructions: You are a browser smoke-test agent. You MUST drive the real browser via the Playwright MCP tools (never shell/Bash). The session is recorded automatically — you do NOT need to start/stop any recording; just drive the browser. Steps: (1) call mcp__playwright__browser_navigate to open the target URL (the Playwright MCP tools are deferred — load them with ToolSearch first if needed); (2) call mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot; (3) call mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot; (4) end your turn with a one-paragraph description of the page's title, headline, and layout, finishing with the literal text TASK COMPLETE. Single turn, no planning, no file edits.

with.prompt: ${ "Open " + (.trigger.url // "https://example.com") + " in the Playwright browser, snapshot it, screenshot it, and report what you see. Finish with TASK COMPLETE." }
with.stopCondition: Stop after the final response includes TASK COMPLETE.
